User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the JTMK+ system, either a student or an admin.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity, matches Firebase Auth UID.

role.description: Role of the user (e.g., student, admin).

course.description: The course the student is enrolled in.

Program.title: Program
Program.description: Represents a JTMK program or event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the program entity.

startDate.description: Start date and time of the program.

endDate.description: End date and time of the program.

imageUrl.description: URL of the program's poster image.

Participation.title: Participation
Participation.description: Represents a user's participation in a program.

id.description: Unique identifier for the participation entity.

studentName.description: Denormalized name of the student for easy display.

programName.description: Denormalized name of the program for easy display.

activityEvidenceUrl.description: URL of the uploaded activity evidence (image).

verificationStatus.description: Status of the activity evidence verification (e.g., pending, approved, rejected).

badgeIssued.description: Indicates whether a badge has been issued for this participation.

certificateIssued.description: Indicates whether a certificate has been issued for this participation.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores program details. Includes 'adminId' for authorization, ensuring only the admin who created the program can modify it.

params.name: programId
params.description: The unique identifier of the program.

definition.description: Stores participation records for each user. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'programId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: participationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the participation record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the JTMK+ application's requirements for program participation, activity evidence verification, and digital certification. The structure prioritizes authorization independence by denormalizing necessary data to avoid `get()` calls in security rules. Segregation is used to maintain a homogeneous security posture within collections.

Authorization Independence:
*   Programs: Each program document will store the `adminId`, to define who can edit it and to make the security rules independent of other collections.
*   Participations: The participations subcollection will store the `userId` and `programId` of each participation to define who can see it and to make the security rules independent of other collections.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters):
*   The structure is designed to support secure list operations. The use of path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/participations/{participationId}`) ensures that users can only list their own participation records.

Access Modeling:
*   Private User Data: User profiles are stored under `/users/{userId}`, ensuring that only the user can access their data.
*   User-Owned Data: Programs and participations are modeled using hierarchical paths (`/programs/{programId}` and `/users/{userId}/participations/{participationId}`) to simplify ownership-based security rules.
